title: Collective bargaining | Employment New Zealand
content:
## Collective bargaining
Collective bargaining is the process used for negotiating and renegotiating collective agreements.
- Collective bargaining process The duty of good faith applies through all the stages of the collective bargaining process. Employment Services mediators can help at any stage of collective bargaining.

## Collective bargaining process
The duty of good faith applies through all the stages of the collective bargaining process. Employment Services mediators can help at any stage of collective bargaining.
- Starting collective bargaining Employers or unions can initiate bargaining by giving the other party or parties an initiation of bargaining notice.

## Starting collective bargaining
Employers or unions can initiate bargaining by giving the other party or parties an initiation of bargaining notice.
- Preparing for bargaining Preparing for collective bargaining may include choosing and training the bargaining team, drafting a potential collective agreement, and setting up the bargaining process agreement.

## Preparing for bargaining
Preparing for collective bargaining may include choosing and training the bargaining team, drafting a potential collective agreement, and setting up the bargaining process agreement.
- Preparing your team Choosing and training the bargaining team in preparation for collective bargaining.
Choosing and training the bargaining team in preparation for collective bargaining.
- Negotiating the agreement Negotiations are part of the collective bargaining process. They refer to any form of discussion, either formal or informal, that is used to reach an agreement.

## Negotiating the agreement
Negotiations are part of the collective bargaining process. They refer to any form of discussion, either formal or informal, that is used to reach an agreement.
- Good faith in collective bargaining Unions and employers who are involved in collective bargaining must deal with each other in good faith.

## Good faith in collective bargaining
Unions and employers who are involved in collective bargaining must deal with each other in good faith.
- Passing on collective agreement terms Passing on collective agreement terms to individual employment agreements.

## Passing on collective agreement terms
Passing on collective agreement terms to individual employment agreements.
- After the negotiations When the bargaining parties have reached agreement, their agreement must be ratified and signed before it is a collective agreement.

## After the negotiations
When the bargaining parties have reached agreement, their agreement must be ratified and signed before it is a collective agreement.
- Unable to agree If the parties can’t agree, they have several options to choose from. These include asking for help from Employment Mediation Services or the Employment Relations Authority (ERA).
If the parties can’t agree, they have several options to choose from. These include asking for help from Employment Mediation Services or the Employment Relations Authority (ERA).
- Screen industry Under the Screen Industry Workers Act 2022, parties can bargain collectively to set minimum terms for work done by screen production workers. Collective bargaining in the screen industry Bargaining for occupational contracts Bargaining for enterprise contracts
Under the Screen Industry Workers Act 2022, parties can bargain collectively to set minimum terms for work done by screen production workers.
- Collective bargaining in the screen industry
- Bargaining for occupational contracts
- Bargaining for enterprise contracts
